info.name: Jadal Platform — Create a Debate (real cycle)
info.description:
The ONE real debate-creation cycle, in order:

1. Admin creates the debate + format + motion — no participants yet.
2. Debate is visible in the app; users register — team (leader/coach) or solo (for random teams) or judge.
3. Admin picks from the registrations: ≥1 judge + exactly 2 teams (real or a random team built from solo applicants) → status `scheduled → announced`.
4. At the motion-reveal time offset, the motion becomes visible — automatic, no API call.
5. At the prep-rooms-open time offset, the two announced teams are randomly assigned proposition/opposition and every member is approved onto that side → status `announced → teams-selected`, prep rooms open — automatic, no API call.
6. In the prep rooms, each side's leader picks the 3 who actually speak + their order (+ reply speaker if the format has one) — already built & tested, included here for completeness.
7. Chair takes the room live and advances through speeches.

**Important — step 5 was a real gap** at the time this collection was written: nothing ever assigned sides after step 3's `announce()`, so debates got stuck at `announced` forever. This has been fixed in `app/Console/Commands/AdvanceDebatesLifecycle.php` (the `debates:tick` scheduled command) — see folder 5's description for exactly what changed.

**How to use**
1. Set collection variables (gear icon → this collection → Variables): `base_url`, `admin_email`, `admin_password`, and the placeholder user ids. This collection does not create user accounts — create them first via `POST /admin/users` or seeders, with the matching `role` (judge / debater).
2. Run **0. Auth** once.
3. Run **1. One-Time Setup** once per environment (format/motion/two teams) — this is infrastructure, not part of the debate cycle itself.
4. Run folders **2 → 3 → 4 → 6 → 7** in order. Folder **5 is not an HTTP call** — read it, then either wait for real time to pass or run `php artisan debates:tick` yourself.

All responses use the envelope `{ success, message, data }` (or `{ success, message, errors }` on failure).

item.name: 0. Auth

exec: console.log('Saved token for user:', res.data.user && res.data.user.email);
console.warn('Login did not return a token — check email/password variables.');

request.description:
What it does: verifies email+password and issues a Sanctum bearer token for whichever user you log in as.

Reflection on the system: creates a `personal_access_tokens` row; no debate data is touched.

Order: always first for whichever role is about to act — admin for folders 1, 3, and the go-live calls; the relevant leader/coach/judge/solo debater for folder 2's registration calls.

item.description: Run this first. Every other request authenticates via the collection-level Bearer token, which this fills in automatically. For the registration calls in folder 2, log in AS the relevant leader/coach/judge/solo-debater instead — either swap {{token}} between calls, or run this request in a separate Postman tab per user.

item.name: 1. One-Time Setup (admin — infrastructure, not part of the cycle)

request.description: Lists existing debate formats (speech length, reply speech y/n, and the motion-reveal / prep-room time offsets that drive the automatic lifecycle in folder 5). Read-only. First result auto-saved to {{format_id}} — skip the create request below if this returns anything.

Creates one `debate_formats` row. The tiny `*_offset_hours` values (0.05h ≈ 3 min, 0.02h ≈ 1 min) are deliberately small so you can watch folder 5's automatic steps happen within minutes when testing, instead of waiting a full day. Admin-only.
Lists existing motions (debate topics). Read-only, paginated (list is under `data.data`). First result auto-saved to {{motion_id}}.
Creates one `motions` row, tagged with the creating user.

Creates a Team with 3 active members. `leader_id` and every id in `members` must already be users with role=debater. The acting user becomes the team's coach (`created_by`) — that's the account the 'get my registerable teams' call in folder 2 will match against for a coach. Auto-saves the new id to {{prop_team_id}}.
Same as 'Create Team — Proposition', for the second team. Auto-saves the new id to {{opp_team_id}}.

item.description: A debate needs an existing DebateFormat and Motion to reference, and the registration scenario needs two existing Teams. This is one-time setup for your environment, not a step in the debate-creation cycle itself. Skip any request whose id you already have.

item.name: 2. Phase 1 — Admin Creates the Debate

body.raw:
{
  "format_id": {{format_id}},
  "motion_id": {{motion_id}},
  "title": "Social Media Ban Debate",
  "description": "Test debate created from Postman.",
  "scheduled_at": "2026-07-02T13:10:00Z",
  "tag": "postman-test"
}

request.description:
What it does: creates the Debate record with no participants.

Reflection on the system: inserts a `debates` row, status='scheduled', a generated unique `livekit_room_name`, `created_by`=the acting admin. No LiveKit room exists yet.

**`scheduled_at` drives everything downstream** — folders 4 and 5's automatic transitions are timed off it, offset by the format's `motion_reveal_offset_hours` / `prep_rooms_open_offset_hours`. Set it a few minutes in the future (paired with the tiny offsets from folder 1) to watch the whole cycle complete in minutes while testing. Must be in the future.

Order: first real step of the cycle. Auto-saves the new debate's id to {{debate_id}}. The debate is now visible to users in the app (any authenticated user can list/see 'scheduled' debates via `GET /debates`).

item.description: The debate shell only — no judges, teams, or debaters yet.

item.name: 3. Phase 2 — Registration (mobile app)

request.description:
What it does: lists the teams the CALLING user leads or coaches, each flagged eligible/ineligible (and why) for this specific debate. A leader normally sees exactly one team (they lead one); a coach may see several and picks which one to register.

Reflection on the system: read-only — no write.

Order: call this first, as the leader/coach, before 'Register — Team' below, to know which team_id to submit.

What it does: the team's leader or coach registers the whole team for this debate in one call.

Reflection on the system: creates one `debate_participants` row (status='pending', side=null, team_id=the team) per active team member, plus one for the team's coach (role='trainer'). Requires debate status='scheduled'; the caller must be the team's leader or its coach.

Order: run once per team you want in the pool (repeat with {{opp_team_id}}, authenticated as that team's leader/coach). These rows are exactly what shows up in 'Get Registrations' and what the admin picks from in phase 3.

item.name: Register — Solo Debater (for a random team)

request.description:
What it does: an individual debater with no team registers themselves. Authenticated AS that debater (role must be 'debater').

Reflection on the system: creates one `debate_participants` row, status='pending', side=null, team_id=null.

Order: repeat for each solo applicant (e.g. authenticated as {{solo_user_id_1}}, {{solo_user_id_2}}, {{solo_user_id_3}}). These are the pool the admin can later combine into a temporary 'random' team in phase 3's announce call.

What it does: a judge registers interest in this debate. Authenticated AS that judge.

Reflection on the system: creates one `debate_participants` row, status='pending', side='judge'.

Note: this is how the judge shows up in 'Get Registrations' for the admin to pick from in phase 3 — but it's informational only. The admin's 'Announce Debate' call approves a judge directly by user_id and does not actually require this row to exist first.

What it does: everyone who has registered so far, split into teams / judges / solo applicants.

Reflection on the system: read-only view over the `debate_participants` rows the requests above create.

Order: this is the exact list the admin uses in phase 3 to decide who to announce. Meaningful only while the debate is still open for registration ('scheduled') — once it's 'announced' the registration window is closed and there's nothing new to see here.

item.description: The debate is visible and open for registration while status='scheduled'. Three ways to register, all via the same endpoint with a different `as` value. Each POST here must be sent authenticated AS that user (leader/coach/judge/solo debater) — log in as them in folder 0 first.

item.name: 4. Phase 3 — Admin Announces the Line-up

request.description:
What it does: admin names ≥1 judge and exactly 2 teams. This variant uses two already-registered real teams.

Reflection on the system: sets `debates.proposition_team_id`/`opposition_team_id` to the two team ids (just placeholder slots for now — NOT the real prop/opp assignment, see folder 5), inserts one `debate_participants` row per team member (role='debater', side=null, status='pending' — the FULL pool, not just 3), and approves the judge(s) with monotonic `judge_order` (first judge listed = chair). Status flips `scheduled → announced` immediately.

Order: run once, after enough registrations exist. This is THE trigger for phase 3 → 4.

item.name: Announce Debate — One Random Team From Solo Applicants (Admin) [alternative]

request.description:
Alternative body for the SAME endpoint — shows the 'random team' case: instead of `team_id`, one entry supplies `user_ids` (the solo applicants from 'Register — Solo Debater', ≥3 of them).

Reflection on the system: the admin creates a real `teams` row on the spot (`is_random=true`, name auto-generated like 'Lineup AB3KD'), with those solo applicants as its members, THEN proceeds exactly as the real-team case above. Each `teams` entry must be exactly ONE of `team_id` or `user_ids`, never both.

This is an alternative to the request above, not a second step — run ONE of the two per debate.

Fetches the full debate detail — status, format, motion, participants, phases, result. Read-only.

Order: run after announcing to confirm `data.status === "announced"` and see the pooled (still side=null) participants before moving to folder 5.

item.description: The admin reviews 'Get Registrations' and picks exactly who's in: ≥1 judge, and exactly 2 teams (each an already-registered real team, or a fresh 'random' team built on the spot from solo applicants). Sides are deliberately NOT chosen here — see folder 5.

item.name: 5. Phases 4 & 5 — Automatic (no HTTP call)
6. Phase 6 — Prep Rooms: Pick Speakers & Order (already built & tested)

request.description:
What it does: sets the SPEAKING ORDER for a side (array order = speaking slot 1/2/3), chosen from that side's already-approved pool. If the format has a reply speech, also include `reply_speaker_user_id` (must be slot 1 or 2).

Reflection on the system: sets `speaking_phase_order` (and `is_reply_speaker`) on the chosen 3 rows. Requires status 'teams-selected' or 'live', and `current_stage === 0`. Only the side's team leader may call it.

Order: after folder 5 completes (status='teams-selected'). Run once per side.
Same as 'Set Team Speakers — Proposition', for the opposing side.

item.description: Once status='teams-selected', each side's leader is in the prep room and picks exactly 3 of their side's approved pool to actually speak, in order (+ the reply speaker, if the format has one). Included for completeness — this part was already working before this session, not touched.

item.name: 7. Go Live

request.description: The single source of truth the frontend polls for a live debate — current stage, both sides' rosters/speakers, judges, timer, server clock. Read-only. Safe to call anytime once the debate exists.

Returns a signed LiveKit JWT for the calling user to join a room (`room`: main | prop | opp | result). No database writes. A real client uses this to connect audio/video; Postman can't join the room, this just proves the token endpoint and inspects the returned grants.

What it does: chair takes the room from the open lobby into the live INTRO screen — still `current_stage=0`, no speech/timer running, just the welcome/chair card.

Reflection on the system: sets `live_started_at` (idempotent after the first call) and broadcasts `debate_mode_started` over the LiveKit data channel. Chair-only, requires status='live' and current_stage=0.

Order: after the debate reaches status='live' (automatic), before the first 'Next Stage' call.

What it does: advances to the next phase — the first call after start-live begins speech #1.

Reflection on the system, per call: closes the previous phase and stops its LiveKit egress recording, if any; opens the next phase and starts a new audio-only (.mp3) recording of the upcoming speaker's mic if they're connected (best-effort, never blocks the request); resets the server-side speech timer; broadcasts `stage_changed` over the LiveKit data channel. Past the last speech it moves into the 'result phase' (status stays 'live') instead of completing — only `close-room` finalises a debate. A rapid second call while one is still processing is rejected with 409 rather than piling up.

Order: call once per speech to drive the debate forward. Chair-only.

item.description: What a chair judge does once the debate reaches status='live' (the `scheduled_at` moment itself — also automatic, via the same `debates:tick` command: it auto-fills any speaking slots you skipped in folder 6, requires ≥1 approved judge, creates the debate_phases rows, provisions the LiveKit room, and flips status → 'live'). All POSTs here must be authenticated AS the chair judge, except the token request.

variable.description: Existing user with role=debater — leads the proposition team
Existing user with role=debater — leads the opposition team
Existing user with role=debater, NOT on any team — for the solo/random-team scenario
Existing user with role=debater, NOT on any team
Existing user with role=debater, NOT on any team
